_description: Pre-researched SEO topics for 404memoryfound.com. Each topic is a deep-dive narrative (3000-5000 words) written by one of two personas (Marcus or Dana). Generator picks 2 per run (1 Marcus + 1 Dana), top of each tier first. Cross off topics as published.
_strategy: Prioritized by search volume vs competition ratio. Tier 1 = high volume, very low long-form competition. Tier 2 = good volume, moderate competition but winnable with narrative depth. Tier 3 = niche authority builders with almost zero competition.

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to Neopets, the Virtual World That Raised a Generation
priority_1_high.angle: Viacom's $160M acquisition, Flash apocalypse, JumpStart mismanagement, 2023 management buyout. Rise-fall-resurrection business story. 200M+ accounts created.

priority_1_high.title: How Doom Changed Everything About PC Gaming and the Internet
priority_1_high.angle: Shareware model revolution ($100K/day from 1% conversion), id Software garage origins, Carmack's 3D engine, cultural panic over violence, LAN party catalyst. The business and culture story, not just gameplay.

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to Screensavers: Flying Toasters, 3D Pipes, and the Art of Doing Nothing
priority_1_high.angle: After Dark franchise ($50 for screensavers), Berkeley Systems, 3D Maze and Pipes in Windows, Johnny Castaway. Why CRT burn-in made them necessary and LCD killed the entire category. Universal shared experience.

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to Club Penguin, Disney's Accidental MMO
priority_1_high.angle: 200M registered users, Disney's $350M acquisition (2007), the shutdown decision (2017), Club Penguin Island failure, fan-run clones and Disney's legal crackdown. Gen Z's defining online childhood.
target_keywords: why did club penguin shut down

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to Clippy, the Paperclip Everyone Loved to Hate
priority_1_high.angle: Microsoft Bob predecessor, Office 97 launch, the user research they ignored, disabled by default in Office XP, Time's 50 worst inventions list. Modern AI assistant parallel makes it timely. The UX lessons Microsoft learned the hard way.

priority_1_high.title: The Original Sims: How a Virtual Dollhouse Became the Best-Selling PC Game Ever
priority_1_high.angle: Will Wright pitched it as architecture sim, EA almost killed it. Surprise demographic (majority female gamers in 2000). 200M+ franchise copies. The pool ladder removal meme. How it accidentally became a social experiment.

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to MiniDisc, Sony's Forgotten Music Format
priority_1_high.angle: Sony spent billions on development. Dominated Japan, flopped in US. DRM restrictions killed it before iPod did. The collector revival and Gen Z TikTok trend. Last device made 2013. A format that was technically right but strategically doomed.

priority_1_high.title: What Happened to Polaroid, the Camera That Died and Came Back
priority_1_high.angle: Edwin Land's invention, $3B peak revenue, 2001 and 2008 bankruptcies, Impossible Project rescue mission buying the last factory, rebrand to Polaroid Originals. Gen Z's instant film revival. Analog in a digital world.

priority_2_medium_high.title: What Happened to Kazaa and the P2P Wars After Napster
priority_2_medium_high.angle: Sharman Networks, FastTrack protocol, $100M RIAA settlement, Australian court case. Creates P2P trilogy with existing Napster and LimeWire posts. The legal and technical evolution of file sharing.

priority_2_medium_high.title: What Happened to Iomega Zip Drives and the Click of Death
priority_2_medium_high.angle: Stock went from $2 to $100 and back. The Click of Death defect and class-action lawsuit. 100MB felt infinite. Killed by CD-R and USB flash drives. A single USB stick now holds 1000x more. The physical ritual of swapping disks.

priority_2_medium_high.title: What Happened to SimCity and How EA Destroyed It
priority_2_medium_high.angle: Will Wright's original vision, SimCity 2000's perfection, SimCity 2013's always-online DRM disaster, server crashes at launch, tiny city sizes, Maxis closure in 2015. How Cities: Skylines ate their lunch. A masterclass in how to kill a franchise.

priority_2_medium_high.title: What Happened to RollerCoaster Tycoon, Built by One Man in Assembly
priority_2_medium_high.angle: Chris Sawyer single-handedly coded RCT in x86 assembly language. 4M+ copies of RCT1. Atari's mismanagement of RCT4/World. Planet Coaster as spiritual successor. One of gaming's most incredible solo development feats.

priority_3_niche_authority.title: What Happened to BonziBuddy, the Internet's Friendliest Spyware
priority_3_niche_authority.angle: The purple gorilla that harvested your data. FTC complaint, $75K fine (that's it). COPPA violations. The Wild West of early internet software. Now a meme icon. Consumer trust in the pre-privacy era.

priority_3_niche_authority.title: What Happened to Oregon Trail, the Game That Taught America to Die of Dysentery
priority_3_niche_authority.angle: Created in 1971 by three student teachers on a teletype. MECC distributed it to every school. 65M+ copies. One-third of MECC's $30M annual revenue. The educational computing revolution. Modern remakes and the dysentery meme.

priority_3_niche_authority.title: What Happened to Newgrounds, the Internet Before YouTube
priority_3_niche_authority.angle: Tom Fulp's creation, Flash animation golden age, Salad Fingers, Alien Hominid going retail, early Egoraptor/Game Grumps. Survived Flash's death. Still running. Where internet culture was actually born before YouTube existed.

priority_3_niche_authority.title: What Happened to IRC, the Chat Protocol That Built the Internet
priority_3_niche_authority.angle: Created by Jarkko Oikarinen in 1988. Gulf War was first event covered on IRC. Channel wars, DCC exploits, the split between EFNet and DALnet. Directly influenced AIM, Slack, Discord, Twitch chat. Still used by open-source communities today.
